I didn't have to wait much. The atmosphere, music - everything was great. This place is a hidden gem, recommend to those who'd like to eat and relax 💎

properties.name: Mlinar
properties.description: A casual Mlinar cafe next to the city market, offering baked goods, pastries, sandwiches, and self-service coffee in a simple indoor seating area. It is a convenient spot for a quick breakfast, snack, or coffee break in the city center.
reviews: Everything I've gotten to eat at any Mlinar has been delicious and a good price. I usually avoid chains, but they're just so good!

Unfortunately the coffee is produced by an automatic machine... So nothing really special The food is okeish, I cannot complain. If you are hungry and you cannot find anything else around, this place will be fine.

Good bakery, but coffe is overpriced (2 eur for coffe in paper cup?). 🙄Price of yoghurt is also quite high (1.8 eur) - I remember the times then normal price was 0.4 eur (few years ago). 🤑 I had a chicken burek here and I was suprised that I liked it more than traditional one ( with beef )

properties.name: La Familia
properties.description: A lively restaurant in the main street of Pula’s old town, known for seafood, fish dishes, pasta, and a pleasant covered terrace in the heart of the historic centre.
reviews: Absolutely amazing restaurant, best food and service I have ever had 😁 No restaurant beats this place, outstanding!!

La Familia is ideal for those who want to eat well and spend the right amount. We loved the typical cuisine and fresh fish. Great place to relax and enjoy good food. Every dish is a surprise for the palate. The portions are carefully prepared and abundant

This feels like a family-run restaurant. We had three courses, and all courses arrived promptly. The restaurant was half full due to it being off peak season. The steak was cooked perfectly, with chips and roasted veg as sides. The Bruiscetta was wonderful and definitely enough for at least 2/3 to share. The young man who served us was friendly and engaging, and overall this place was a real gem. Would definitely come back!

La Familia restaurant has the most fresh and delicious food. We enjoyed everything we ordered. Great location. Just be aware that they only take cash and the restroom is not the cleanest in my opinion.

properties.name: Furia Restaurant
properties.description: A local seafood spot inside Pula’s market building, Furia is known for fresh, fast, and affordable fish dishes and simple Adriatic specialties.
reviews: A bit spartan, but very good. Fish definitely fresh and very tasty. Friendly and friendly staff.
Very freshly prepared, very tasty and at a more than fair price on an outdoor terrace. Highly recommended!!

4 stars. Located on the top floor of the marketplace building. Offers seating inside the building or on the balcony. Food was nice and fresh. Understandably not everything on the menu was in season / available. We had the octopus salad and a couple of pieces of fish. Fresh and cooked well. Washed down with a lovely glass of local white wine. €30 for 2 people. Can't fault the value. Happily recommend this place.

A place to eat fish in centre of town. They have wild fish in daly offer, as well as grown. Prices are totally acceptable. The staff is amazing, chef is encrideble! Sometimes you have to wait for the table a while, but it is completely worth it. Every time we ate here it was great 🤍🌊🌞

properties.name: Street Food Two
properties.description: A modern street food spot in the city center of Pula, offers fresh, colorful, and healthier fast meals, with plenty of salads, bowls, burritos, and vegetarian options.
reviews: Fresh, delicious and surprisingly versatile – highly recommended! The food was consistently fresh and top-quality – you immediately notice that they work with high-quality ingredients. The Service was super friendly, attentive and competent. We were particularly surprised by the selection of vegetarian dishes, which is really great by Croatian standards. Vegan options are also available and lovingly prepared. A place that perfectly combines pleasure, quality and hospitality.

As a vegetarian finding healthy, vegan and vegetarian options in Croatia is simply too rare to be picky. However, this spot is simple, very nice and the food super fine. We had the tofu poke and the kids the tortellini. Everyone was happy. The staff was extremely friendly!
Some great fresh healthy options here and amazing tasting food. We had the chickpea patties and tofu poke bowl which was so delicious! Flavours were fab & lovely staff!

Yum, yum, yum. So fresh and cooked with love. Our first time trying this (we are new to Pula and it's close to our apartment) and it really delivered . I had the prawn and pumpkin risotto and my husband ordered the beef burrito. The taste was great and the portion large, the price reasonable for how much food you get. The service was positive and the server was smiling and happy. Quick stop, we hardly waited. We will be back. Thank you for a lovely, healthy lunch.

properties.name: Restaurant Boschetto
properties.description: A relaxed restaurant in Verudela, close to the beach, serves Mediterranean and Istrian cuisine with a focus on fresh fish, seafood, pasta, and local specialties in a green, peaceful setting.
reviews: Perfect place in the the forest shade for lunch/dinner. Good food and good prices. No fine dining, but the sea food and fish and pizzas are excellent. Service staff is very friendly and accomodating.

We were there a few times during our stay in Verudela. The food was tasty and the portions were big enough! You have a plenty of choice for different dishes, pizza, calamari, octopus, fish ….also the waiters were polite and friendly. I recommend it!

We were very hungry after spending the whole morning at Ambrela beach and visiting Aquaria, so restaurant Boschetto was the first option. The restaurant was full, only a few free seats in the sun. But after a while we were able to move. The menu is extensive - pizza, meat, fish, salads, seafood. We had a small beer, I had tagliatelle with seafood and my boyfriend chose cevabcici with fries and ajvar. The portions were large, the prices very reasonable. The staff took very good care of us, thank you very much.

properties.name: Bistro Alighieri
properties.description: A casual bistro in Pula’s old town with a central square terrace, serves Mediterranean and Istrian dishes, daily lunch menus, fish, meat, pasta and salads in a relaxed city setting.
reviews: A lovely little bistro near the fountain square. We had a wonderful lunch here and tried the traditional noodles with truffle sauce - absolutely delicious. We also ordered Istrian red wine, which I can definitely recommend. The staff were lovely and the service was ok. The atmosphere was good.

This platter was delicious and beautifully presented. The mix of cheeses, cured meats, olives, and fresh tomatoes was perfect. Everything tasted fresh and well-balanced, and the crunchy breadsticks added a nice touch. A great appetizer to share — simple, tasty, and satisfying.

Absolutely amazing food and service at Bistro Alighieri in Pula! We enjoyed perfectly cooked calamari, baby octopus, and the local pasta Pljukanci with creamy truffle sauce. The tuna cappuccio starter and Croatian wine made it a perfect evening. Highly recommended!

The best Cuttlefish Risotto I've ever tasted. It was so good, I forgot to photograph it. The restaurant service is great, food is hot and freshly cooked. Serving staff are friendly. This is a restaurant where the food quality speaks for itself. Nice prices, excellent food. Simple

properties.name: Piantina Fine Fast Food
properties.description: A casual fast food spot in Pula serving burgers, grilled sandwiches, salads and fries, with a menu that mixes classic comfort food with a few more creative options.
reviews: The food was delicious and the Service was great. We felt very comfortable.The burgers are fried before our eyes. The salads and bread are also fresh and very delicious. Gluten-free options are also available.

Good location in the centre of pula parking easy to find we found parking immediately. Very cute and nice tables and pretty inside. The burger and the salad were amazing! I recommend and the waiter was really nice and welcoming😁
14.90€ for a combo: Burger of choice, french fries, and a drink of choice. I went with a 'smoker' and it was so good. The patty was juicy, and the fries were fine.

We are there for 5 adults and 3 young children on a rainy Monday and we had a great time. The plates are quite generous ! We ate hamburgers, cheeseburger, honey chicken sandwich and a chicken salad with some fries and it was good. The cook was very friendly with the kids and even gave us a free iced tea. Thank you again for that !

properties.name: Nimfej
properties.description: A local no-frills bar right on the parking area in front of the Arena, popular for coffee, beer and a casual drink with an open view of Pula’s most famous landmark.
reviews: A café at a parking lot what can you expect? I wasn’t sure whether to give 5 or 3 stars, since it doesn’t really meet certain standards. But honestly, if you don’t expect anything extra, you’ll be satisfied: great view of the theater, excellent coffee, fast service. For me, it’s 5 stars. I didn’t expect anything special.
I guess the Cafe with the best views of the Theatre.👌🏻
Amazing place! As a tourist this was amazing! I would definitely go back again!

properties.name: Adi
properties.description: A simple city-centre fast food spot best known for its kebabs, with quick service and classic takeaway options like tortillas, burgers and fries.
reviews: Food was great. Service was fast and personalized. They accommodated my food allergy. No air conditioning so its a little hot inside.
Really good kebab, one of the best in Pula.
Favorite kebab spot in Pula! The food is excellent, the lepinja especially is very good here. The spot is no frills takeout, but always extremely clean and very fast. Cheap too! We love Kebab Adi!

properties.name: Batak
properties.description: Modern grill restaurant in Pula City Mall offering a wide range of meat dishes, steaks and comfort food in a stylish, contemporary setting.
reviews: Very good food and excellent service. We had ćevapi and the stuffed pork steak. The ćevapi were juicy and well grilled, with soft lepinja. The stuffed pork steak was rich and well prepared. Homemade potatoes with melted cheese and crispy bacon were a great side. Apple tart was a solid dessert, not too sweet. Service was the highlight. Fast, professional, and friendly. Make a reservation, this place gets busy.

Everything we had was amazing! Everything. You must do reservation over web page. Fancy but with very REASONABLE prices! Quality! I was more than surprised over the whole package! You cannot compare this prices and quality to some / most restaurants in Pula. We’ve been in Zagreb in one od your restaurants couple of years ago, but this one left us speechless. Compliments 😻👏🏻🥇

We went in for a light meal, food was fantastic. What stands out even more was their service. Extremely friendly and kind service! We really loved the time spent there. Interiors were cozy and elegant! And in the end, they also gave a little peppa pig present for my little daughter!

properties.name: Restoran lesnina XXXL
properties.description: A casual self-service shopping restaurant in Lesnina offering hearty everyday meals like schnitzels, burgers, grilled dishes, salads, pasta, fish and simple desserts at accessible prices.

properties.name: Old City Bar
properties.description: A pub located in the center of Pula, offering a relaxed atmosphere during the day and lively parties at night. It is a popular spot for drinks, socializing, and weekend nightlife.
reviews: Great service, Marcus was attentive and funny and made our night!! Drinks were all very good and really enjoyed the theatrics of the different shots and cocktails.
Best Cocktails in town and super cool service personal, Marcus is our man! Come here! ✨🙌

The service in this place cannot be compared to anything I've experienced so far of my nine days in Croatia. Not the cheapest, but the persistence and effort by Marco is why you pay a premium. The live music was also quite good. Awsome Bloody Mary if you're into that, and a selection of craft beer if that is your thing. (To Marco's employer, you should give this man a raise)

properties.name: Konoba Veruda
properties.description: A traditional tavern in Veruda serving authentic Istrian cuisine in a warm, rustic setting. The menu focuses on homemade pasta, goulash, grilled meat, seafood, truffle dishes, and other local specialties, making it a great place to try classic regional food.
reviews: The atmosphere of the place, ambient, and friendliness of our waitress was so wonderful. But even more impressive was the amazing food! Marvelous experience

We took a chance on this place and were completely blown away by the quality and quantity of food. Our final dinner in Pula before heading home was amazing. The steaks and fish dishes are superior and everything prepared with care and attention. The mangold side (which we thought was spinach) is devine. We were treated to free shots of the home-brewed liquor before leaving. Fabulous!

If you're visiting Pula and looking for a spot with great service, generous portions, and good value for money, this restaurant is a must-try. Conveniently located near our hotel, it offered a welcoming atmosphere. While it's not within walking distance of the city center, it's easily accessible via bus 2A, taking only about 10-15 minutes. The food was delicious, with portions larger than expected, making it perfect for those with a hearty appetite. Overall, this place combines quality dining with affordability, making it a top recommendation for anyone visiting Pula.

properties.name: Hum
properties.description: A family-run cafe in the city center, popular for morning coffee and casual drinks.
reviews: Best coffee we had in Pula and our trip in Croatia! Decaf coffee was also excellent. We went every morning of our stay after breakfast. The service was really good too. The two men servers were very kind and by the 3rd day knew our order and asked if we wanted our usual! Highly recommend!
Great spot for a quick coffee, and it was not a bad one which is great. Normal price, quick service and lots of spots to seat. Yes to this place.
Sat down and had coffee after visiting the market nearby. Clean toilets, good coffee (also decaf options), friendly service! The shaded terrace helped with the heat 😊.

properties.name: Fabbrica
properties.description: A modern restaurant in old town, set in a renovated former boiler house with a distinctive industrial atmosphere. Fabbrica is known for Neapolitan pizza, BBQ dishes, burgers, craft beer, and a creative menu that blends authentic Italian flavors with local Istrian ingredients.
reviews: A perfect place for a dinner with friends after a day at the beach! After a little walk from the Arena trough a busy central area on a summer Friday evening we stopped at this unique restaurant. We got a big table at the corner inside nearby the garden. The ventilation, the lights, the overall design with attention to details like beauty of natural field flowers in big vases and vintage style glasses. I have ordered the fish plate which contained of 3 differently graduated seafood, from sweet&sour marinated sepia to salty anchovies. A glass of sparkling wine married perfectly this gastronomic experience. It was something different from what I could ate in other restaurants at the coast. Compliments to the waiters service, so attentive yet discret. Highly recommended!
Only popped in for a beer but a stunning eatery. Huge selection of beers. Very clean and service was excellent.

Lovely local craft beers, spanning from Zagreb to Istria. The ambiance of their back garden is incredible, with views of a beautiful tree, fresh herbs, and freshly cut logs for the wood fire pizza oven. Highly recommend this restaurant for great service and a chill vibe. It’s also dog friendly!

properties.name: Pulačinka
properties.description: A cozy dessert and pancake cafe in the center of Pula, known for sweet and savory pancakes, waffles, churros, and other treats. Guests can customize their own pancake with a wide range of spreads, toppings, fruit, biscuits, and chocolate bars, making it a popular spot for breakfast, dessert, or a casual snack.
reviews: We had very tasty dessert here, a giant pancake and a wafle. Both were packed with sweet things full of calories 😀 You can select from pre-set variations of sweet or salty pancakes, or you can create your own version.

This creperie is absolutely amazing! The pistachio sauce is the best I’ve ever had – rich, creamy, and full of flavor. On top of that, the staff are incredibly friendly and attentive, which makes the whole experience even better. Highly recommended!

We have tried churros and custom pancake. Churros were good, but not the best that we had. But pancake was almost perfect. It was done quickly without saving on ingredients. There are many different ingredients to choose from and you can mix as much as you want. Prices are high but it is normal for Pula I guess..

I've eaten here a palačinka with my girlfriend and it was the most Nutella I ate in a long time. Then, she had a blood sugar spike so powerful she drove 360 km without any pause. Recommended before long road trips 👍🏻

properties.name: Konoba Bocun
properties.description: A traditional tavern surrounded by greenery and close to Šijana Forest. Konoba Bocun offers a blend of traditional Istrian and international cuisine, with homemade pasta, truffle dishes, grilled specialties, creative seasonal meals, and a strong selection of quality local wines in a warm, authentic setting.
reviews: This place is located outside the chaos of the city and it is really a shame that it does not enjoy wide visibility. The location is very nice and cared for, the staff is very kind and attentive, you eat good fish (I did not taste the meat so I can not say). Prices are in line with other restaurants. I recommend it if you do not want too much confusion and eat in peace

Konoba Bocun was a wonderful surprise. The food was absolutely delicious, fresh, and full of authentic flavors with some twist. Service was super, super friendly and fast, making us feel welcome and relaxed the whole time.

We were pleasently surprised with this restaurant, from the road you cannot say it has such great atmosphere. The stuff is super nice and food delicious. We had tuna stake that melted on the mouth and home made gnocchi with deer, 2 glasses of teran, bottled water and home made bread for 45 eur. We will certainly visit again.
A perfect meal. The salad with cuttlefish was amazing also the sea bass filled with shrimp and spinach + the dessery with truffles was really really good! Perfect experience

properties.name: Popaj
properties.description: A cozy restaurant in old town known for grilled meat, fresh seafood, and classic Croatian dishes. With its charming outdoor terrace, friendly service, and menu featuring fish platters, tuna steak, calamari, and local wine, it is a great spot for a relaxed meal in the historic center.
reviews: Great location, no waiting time at dinner (7pm). Staff is very good and the atmosphere was nice, pretty quiet outside. We took meat plate and calmari, which had no taste but sauces gave them some flavours. The mussel was good.

Very good service, waiter and waitress are really kind and speak multiple languages, English and Italian very well! Food amazing, very fulfilling and well served and tasty! Calamari in different ways are always very soft and so so good! They do as well this honey grappa flavoursome! Thank you so much!

we kinda stumbled upon this place while searching for a restaurant to feast for dinner. its nestled in a little corner so there’s space for privacy if you prefer it, ambiance is pretty top notch. the food was not too bad, but the lack of variety and not-so-crispy calamari takes a star away from a perfect 5 rating. the wait staff and owners were very pleasant and friendly though, we really enjoyed their service!

properties.name: Kinema
properties.description: A modern pub and restaurant in Pula with a lively social atmosphere, combining great food, drinks, and entertainment in one place. Kinema is known for billiards, darts, live sports screenings, themed events, burgers, steaks, breakfast, local wines, cocktails, and craft beer, making it a popular spot for both casual hangouts and nights out with friends.
reviews: Fantastic bar/restaurant. Visiting from England I was looking for somewhere to watch the Aston Villa match, walked in I believe I met the owner, said he had the game on, minute later a guy came over and said you want the Villa game on the big screen, yes I said, that’s great hospitality! The bar is extremely cool, strangely it smells wonderful in there, darts, pool all can be played. Food is excellent, we had a grazing board and chicken wings, spot on they was. Staff, really friendly and welcoming, sorry about this as I couldn’t remember her name, but Drag….. was first class.

One of the best restaurants in Pula, especially if you’re looking for a place to eat near the Pula Arena. The food portions were huge, definitely more than enough for one person, and we left feeling very full and satisfied. The drinks were excellent and had a classy touch. The ambiance was fantastic, with great entertainment and we didn’t even realize we had stayed for over 3 hours! Highly recommended.

What a venue! If you are looking for live sports, pool, darts or just a good atmosphere, head here. An old cinema has been transformed into a superb sports bar. A small doorway opens up to this vast multi room venue, and the screen is maaaasssssiiiiiiiivvveee. A nice welcome to fast knowledgeable service & they go to every effort to accommodate your needs. After the large main room, there's a pool room off to one side & another more intimate balcony area upstairs. The atmosphere is not too loud, so you have to shout, and the other guests are all smiling and up to banter. The menu does not do justice to the food, which was excellent. A mix of burgers, etc, to more traditional dishes Generous portios, to say the least, could not even look at the desserts menu. A real gem * kids are welcome

This was the best restaurant I've visited in Pula. Pavle, the server was extremely pleasant and welcoming, he gave some great suggestions. I came in only to have some coffee but ended up getting the breakfast and dessert. Couldn't tell which was better. Extremely well made food, with even better staff. I couldn't recommend this place more!

properties.name: Pizzeria Piccolo Porto
properties.description: A cozy pizzeria in old town serving authentic Neapolitan pizza in a warm and inviting setting. Known for its long-fermented dough, Italian ingredients, and classic pizzas like Margherita and Diavola, it is a great spot for a true taste of Naples in the city center.
reviews: We've enjoyed lunch here on 3 days. The reasons we kept returning; plenty of choice with traditional dishes to the region on the menu, the food is delicious, waiter Eva is welcoming and really helpful in guiding us through the menu, the lunch price is great value. Toilets were very clean too. Highly recommend.

Hands down the best Neapolitan pizza in town! I’ve tried every pizzeria around, and nothing comes close to this gem. The ingredients are super fresh, the dough is light, soft in the center, and perfectly crispy on the edges, just like true Neapolitan tradition. Every bite is a burst of authentic flavors! The staff is mega friendly, and the vibe of the street is amazing. If you’re craving real Neapolitan pizza, this is THE spot. Highly recommend! 🍕🔥
Truly the best pizza I have ever eaten — not an exaggeration. The owner is so nice and accommodating, and the food is fresh, local, and delicious. You have to come here if you are in Pula.
Great pizza with good choice of salad and also dishes like kalamari, fish and chips and fried chicken cutlets right in the heart of the city!

properties.name: Kavana Lattiera
properties.description: A modern cafe in Veruda known for specialty coffee, healthy breakfasts, smoothie bowls, homemade cakes, and a relaxed neighborhood atmosphere. Inspired by Berlin-style coffee culture, it is a popular spot for morning coffee, brunch, fresh juices, and casual meetups on the square.
reviews: We stopped at Kavana Lattiera and really enjoyed the atmosphere. The coffee and desserts were excellent, and the place has a very nice vibe for sitting down and relaxing. One thing that stood out, though, was the wait time – we had to wait more than 30 minutes for our order, and it felt like we had been forgotten for a while. Once everything finally arrived, the quality made up for it, but the service could definitely be more attentive.

Great place with a fresh and healthy offer! The menu is full of delicious options made from quality ingredients — from smoothie bowls and sandwiches to great breakfast choices. The açaí bowl is especially tasty and definitely worth trying. Perfect spot for a relaxed coffee or a healthy meal.

During the vacation with my girlfriend (who only eats vegetarian and gluten free) in Croatia, we went a couple of times here. What a nice place and so many locals went there as well. The menu was only on Croatian and we had to translate but that was not a problem at all. The service, the food, everything is so nice here. And the best thing is, it’s open until 4 pm.

Very interesting establishment set amongst apartments and shops. Inside it's a whole different feel to the outside and to what I have seen in Pula, which is very comforting and reminds me of other international cafes, and those special couple of cafes in Zagreb too. The food is so good, healthy and very thoughtful recipes. Veg will be happy. Oat milk available.

properties.name: Amore Pizza & Pasta
properties.description: A charming pizza and pasta restaurant in old town, just behind the Temple of Augustus. Known for homemade pasta, authentic Neapolitan pizza, fresh local ingredients, and a cozy atmosphere near the harbor, it is a great choice for a relaxed Italian-style meal in a historic setting.
reviews: Simple, delicious, and affordable. The dough is light and crispy, tomato sauce is flavorful, and toppings are generous. We had a Margherita and a Diavola – both came out piping hot from the wood-fired oven. Service was friendly and fast. Two pizzas + two drinks cost us around €25. A solid choice if you're craving authentic pizza without tourist prices.

At Pizzeria Amore we ordered a pizza with stracciatella and homemade pappardelle with beef. The pizza was delicious – true Neapolitan style, with a perfect crust and a rich tomato sauce. The pasta was cooked to perfection as well. However, the prices compared to portion sizes are about twice as high as what you would normally pay just across the border in Italy. Since Croatia adopted the euro, dining out has become noticeably more expensive.

The best Neapolitan pizza in Pula. We had a big appetite for pizza and we really liked the outdoor garden behind the church of St. Augustus. We had lemon lemonade and iced tea. I chose a pizza with green pesto, mozzarella, mortadella with truffles and pistachios. Incredible taste and a great combination. I even drizzled it with chili oil and I was the happiest. My boyfriend chose a pizza with a tomato base, mozzarella, Isterian sausage and on top he had a little bit of cheese mixed with cream. Perfect choice and we really enjoyed it. Thank you very much.

We had an amazing dinner at Amore Pizzeria! The pizza and pasta were fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented — you can really taste the quality ingredients. The atmosphere in the Old Town courtyard was cozy and charming, and the staff were super friendly and attentive. Great wine list too! Definitely a must-visit in Pula.

properties.name: Pizzeria LEMA
properties.description: A casual neighborhood pizzeria in Pula, best known for pizza delivery but also offering indoor and outdoor seating and a full menu for dine-in. The menu focuses on a wide range of classic and specialty pizzas, along with pasta, grilled meat dishes, salads, fish, and simple desserts, making it a convenient choice for both a quick meal and takeout.
reviews: This place came recommended by our B&B host. We love a good spicy pizza do we ordered a Diavolo as a walk-in for carryout. The pizza was very good, but I wish they would cut the spicy peppers to help distribute them better. We did that ourselves which wasn't an issue. They have a nice little operation and can really churn out the pizza at a good pace. Everyone that was working genuinely seemed like they were having a good time. I think we maybe waited 15-20min. They also have a good selection of beers. I grabbed a couple of Leffe.

If you're looking for a place to get pizza from in Pula then look no further because this is it. Great hard working and friendly staff, nice atmosphere and an amazing pizza. This is definitely a place that I'd visit every time I'm here in Pula. Absolutely great

Very nice modern place. The pizza is made with excellent ingredients, baked in an old school fireplace, like it's suppose to be. You have three sizes of pizza available, small, medium and jumbo. The medium size is perfect for one hungry person. Jumbo is perfect for three people. I Highly recommend this excellent pizza place! :) The photo is of a medium (classic pizza size) and only 8euros, i was blown away!
Excellent pizza, one medium pizza is enough for 2 adults. Also pasta carbonara was delicious.

properties.name: Aperitivo bar Galerija
properties.description: A charming cafe located right above the Pula Arena, ideal for breakfast, coffee, cocktails, drinks, or ice cream with a unique historic backdrop. Its setting next to the ancient amphitheater makes it a great stop for a relaxed break in one of the city’s most iconic spots.
reviews: A beautiful place next to the walls of the Great Arena. The service was very friendly and fast, and the food was even better. We had breakfast and got coffee with it and freshly squeezed orange included in the price, the croissants were warm and wonderful with cappuccino. Really worth a visit, all compliments and wonderful experience 🥐
Friendly service. Cool atmosphere. There's a restroom inside. 🚻

And it's great here. We climbed up and ordered ice cream for the kids. The ice cream was super tasty and the portions were large. The service was friendly. The garden by the Colosseum was great. I recommend it.

properties.name: Kebab & Burger Pula
properties.description: A casual kebab and burger bar near the Pula Arena, known for XXL portions, freshly prepared meat, and quick, satisfying street food. The menu includes kebabs, tortillas, burgers, falafel options, fries, and drinks, making it a convenient stop for a fast meal close to one of the city’s main landmarks.
reviews: Big portions and delicious food! We had the veggie falafel tortillas and veggie burgers — both were super tasty and fresh. The fries were perfectly crisp. Definitely worth a visit!
Great Donair restaurant near arena. There is some tables inside to eat at if not doing take out. The portions are huge and the prices are very reasonable. Highly recommend.
One of the best chicken kebabs I ever did eat. Lovely lads working in the grill and the food is great. Never thought I would have one of my best kebabs in Pula. But, hey! These guys do it so well.
Amazingly tasty chicken kebabs, very very big portions, and the sweet and spicy sauce was delicious! Staff were lovely and funny, and quick! Would absolutely go again on next visit

properties.name: Osteria Food & Wine
properties.description: A cozy restaurant and wine bar near the Pula Arena, offering Roman-style pinsa, tapas-style dishes, and a wide selection of Istrian wines. Set in the shadow of the ancient amphitheater, it combines local ingredients, a relaxed atmosphere, and a warm setting that works well for both summer evenings and cozy winter dining.
reviews: Very nice restaurant with terrace and beautiful interior. You can also buy ice cream there. The food was tasty and they separated pizza to two plates, which was good. The toilets are upstairs and you can have a sit there with a nice view. The bar has also many nice bottles

Great restaurant 100 feet from the Pula Arena, the pizza is very good and the Pivo was also very good, the service was very good but it did take 30 mins for our food to come out. Our server provided us with an after meal drink at no charge for the delay.
Everything was perfect, very unique menu and foods. Waiters are very kind and helpful.

The food was absolutely lovely, and the service was outstanding. The waiter Adi was extremely nice and polite with a good sense of humour. We had a fantastic time. We would definitely recommend this place👌

properties.name: Zlatna Vrata
properties.description: A casual restaurant on the main street in old town, offering a varied menu of pizza, pasta, grilled dishes, seafood, and breakfast options. With a central location and relaxed atmosphere, it is a convenient spot for a simple meal while exploring the historic center.
reviews: Perfect food, lovely staff always friendly and great service, love this place. The best icecream in Croatia. We will visit here everytime we are in pula.
Our server was very nice and the portion sizes were more than ok. The food was pretty good and came super quick. We can definitely recommend this place.
Visited this restaurant twice whilst we were staying in Pula and both times we couldn’t fault the food or the service. Highly recommend.
Good tasty pizza, great location, friendly staff and great service

properties.name: Pizza alla Nonna
properties.description: A casual pizzeria in the city center, popular for pizza by the slice as well as whole pizzas. It is a convenient spot for a quick bite, especially for people looking for simple, affordable pizza in a relaxed setting.
reviews: Found this place during sight seeing tour! Only 5 minutes by foot away from the popular amphi-theater! Outstanding place with delicious housemadePizza! Prices are really reasonsble!!! Friendly stuff too!
the pizza is huge and delicious! €2 for a massive slice (see picture:)) the staff were friendly. there is some seating inside but not loads, it’s clean and open - a toilet too i think
By far the best en biggest pizza we ever had. For a fair price (€15 for a big big one) would recommend to anyone💪🏼

properties.name: Maximilian
properties.description: A modern pizzeria in Max City shopping center, known for authentic Neapolitan pizza with long-fermented dough and high-quality ingredients. With a spacious interior, large terrace, and a menu that includes both classic and gourmet pizzas, it is a popular spot for pizza lovers in Pula
reviews: Very delicious pizza! Good choice of different pizzas and drinks. Service is very nice and friendly. Smiling and helping! Speaking English without any problems. Recommend to everyone! Delicious and good price 😊

It's very nice. Not a particular ambient, being in a mall, it is quite a simple restaurant, but the pizza is great and interesting - some flavours we've never tried before. If you like very traditional pizza you might not be as interested, or take the capricciosa, but if you are up for experimenting with taste, take one of the Italian specialties - mortadela salami and pistachio pesto.

Wonderful place to eat pizza! They also have gluten-free pizzas that are amazing! Really good in taste, the service was nice, and they were really fast. The staff was really nice too! I highly recommend this place! (I took the glue free pizza with mortadella that was really good ! )
Amazing Pizza for a good price

properties.name: Barbados
properties.description: A relaxed cafe in Max City shopping center, ideal for short breaks, long coffee sessions, and casual meetups. With a sunny terrace and laid-back atmosphere, it is a nice place to enjoy coffee, drinks, or a quiet moment with a book or newspaper.
reviews: Great spot for coffee, they also have a fantastic cake selection, I like to come here every time I'm at the mall. The staff is very kind, especially the young female waiter and the young male waiter, both so so kind. I reccomand this spot to anyone passing through, by far the best coffee in the mall
Great service. Katarina was very helpful and kind. 5/5
They have real milkshakes made with any flavor ice cream they have! Wonderful

properties.name: Agrippina Street Food
properties.description: A lively street food restaurant in the center of old town, close to the main square. It is known for creative burgers, BBQ dishes, cocktails, and a casual atmosphere, making it a popular spot for quick meals, drinks, and relaxed evenings in the historic center.
reviews: Food was great, probably the best burgers in the city. Considering the location, popularity and pactfulness of the place it is logical why there is a longer waiting period for the table and food, but in the end it pays off. I'd definetly encourage anyone to book the place a several days in advance should you plan on coming in the afternoon/evening hours.
Coming back 3 Years in a row,every year some new things on the menu! I tried this year smashed caligula with homemade strawbery jam,great comination of flavours,Well diserved first place!
Agrippina Imeprial burger is out of this world,wagyu steak is just melting! Very friendly staff and good vibes! I will be back defintly
Great food, very good burgers (a lot of choice) and fries. Very polite and super friendly service. Dogs friendly. Central location.

Great atmosphere and very welcoming staff. The food was fresh, tasty, and nicely presented, with portions just right. Service was quick and friendly, and the prices were fair for the quality. Definitely a place I’d recommend and visit again.

properties.name: Katarina
properties.description: A newer waterfront restaurant in the Vallelunga area of Pula, known for its spacious terrace and beautiful panoramic view over the city and the waterfront. The menu features a wide range of seafood, grilled fish, steaks, meat specialties, pizzas, and premium dishes, making it a great choice for a relaxed lunch or a scenic dinner.
reviews: Beautiful food and lovely stuff would recommend it for lunch. We had the platter and Adriatic squid with local wine. The waitress was lovely and attentive
Hi, we had a steak. It was delicious. We enjoyed the evening with the sunset. The waiter (lady) was very kind.
Beautiful ambient, nice staff and nice food, but the price is a bit more expensive.

The Location is great and the staff very friendly and helpful. The menu sounded promising, but we were disappointed by the food. It looked great but the taste was not so good, we were missing flavors. If they would improve their food it could become the best restaurant in pula.

properties.name: Backyard Restaurant
properties.description: A stylish fine dining restaurant hidden just behind the Arena in Pula, offering a more refined and creative culinary experience than the city’s usual casual dining spots. Known for its tasting menus, elegant service, carefully selected wines, and intimate courtyard setting, it is a great choice for a special dinner focused on modern Mediterranean cuisine and seasonal ingredients.
reviews: It was an amazing experience at Backyard: we tried three “short” menus. Every combination of flavour was made with fresh ingredients, skills and knowledge, in a not obvious way of cooking. 3 menus = 150€ more or less + the lobster. The staff was super kind and on point. You should go there.
Very good and beautiful food. Especially the little appetizer. The shrimp. For gourmets

Minimalist presentation, thoughtful flavors, and a perfect balance of textures. Every plate at Backyard feels like a small piece of art, but more importantly, the taste lives up to the look. Precise, seasonal, and full of character. A place that truly knows what it’s doing. 🍽️✨

Backyard Trattoria is a wonderful (the best of Pula) restaurant near the arena of Pula. For people loving gastronomic food, this is the place to be. We took the degustation menu, however you can also choose for two smaller menu´s. Also the wine is fantastic. The sommelier knows his wines and listens very carefully to understand your expectations to serve you the best wine. For me, they deserve a place in the Gault Millau or Michelin!

properties.name: Mletačka utvrda
properties.description: French-style defensive fortress built by the Venetians in the 1600s, with views of the city & bay.
reviews: Amazing views from the top - panoramic of the whole site - was helpful as my first look of the city to get my bearings. Loved that the ticket also included use of the undercity tunnel network - helpful as it was raining on and off (visited a couple of days ago!)
Very beautiful views. Nice fortress, with a tower from where the views are even more wonderful. The entrance fee is 7 euros.

I like history and I've been to quite a few other fortresses and the design follows other fortress designs because you're trying to defend and required similar designs and systems. The fortress has been disarmed and the museums/displays on site are bright with information and displays but I found some lack.....that is not a bad comment, just a minor con. There has obviously been a lot of work to maintain the integrity of the stone work and that is definitely a pro. Definitely worth the time for a visit. I rate this 4 stars.

properties.name: Fort Valmarin
properties.description: Large 19th-century hilltop fortress above Pula, closed to visitors and surrounded by a defensive ditch.
reviews: Closed area. Ministry of Defence. Located 350 meters from the construction material warehouse and store on Labinska Street...

properties.name: Fort Bourguignon
properties.description: 19th-century coastal fortress in Pula, known for its circular design, military history, and cultural events.
reviews: Beautiful Austro-Hungarian fortress which is preserved, but abandoned. In my opinion the most beautiful one in Pula. Unfortunately it is closed for visitors. I recommend to visit in early morning (between 6:00-7:00 AM) because there are not any people around and sunlight is perfect for photography.

Very nice place to visit. Not crowdy. 10 minutes slow walk from free parking space on front of resort ( on right side from the main gate). To reach te place we used wide route on right side of resort entrance on the bushes.

properties.name: Utvrda Marie Louise
properties.description: Large 19th-century coastal fortress on the Muzil peninsula in Pula, known for its circular design and strategic views over the bay entrance.
reviews: The remains of a large defensive fort on Musil peninsula. It was built in the 19th century and used by different armies throughout history. Today it is almost forgotten but still far from a mere ruin. The strength of its walls and beauty of its architecture are still evident. It is safe to explore, yet watch your step for scrap metal, holes and other obstacles. Just like Verudela this ruin should be restored and put to good use.
Spectacular abandoned fort. You can explore everything. Be careful.

There's a good, convenient entrance, but it's guarded. Closed to the curious. However, you can access the area along the western coast along a narrow path. Along the way, you'll encounter abandoned former military fortifications. It's a bit scary! Once inside, I felt like a stalker! I saw several huge peacocks. This was once a huge military base. Now everything is abandoned, but the buildings, both above and below ground, are in excellent condition.

properties.name: Fort Punta Christo
properties.description: Circular fort built in the late 1800s with underground rooms, also a venue for music festivals.

properties.name: Fort Monte Munide
properties.description: Small 19th-century circular artillery tower above Štinjan, surrounded by forest and known for its military history and quiet hilltop setting.
reviews: One of the 19th century artillery forts around Pula. The remains of it to be exact as it was bombarded in WW II and never reconstructed or conserved since. There is a worn-out info panel and a narrow path to the inside of the fort which is overgrown in trees and bush. There is nothing special to see, only to witness the utmost negligence and incompetence of the locals towards their own legacy. Dissapointing.
Nice example of historical forts unfortunately blown up durning ww2

Whole hill is full of military tunnels and bunkers. At the top is historycal Austrohungarian 19. century fort, 50 meters away is entrance to Yugoslavian era bunker, and right bellow the hill towards sea is another large bunker

properties.name: Camp Brioni
properties.description: Seaside campsite near Pula set in a pine forest, with a long pebble beach, views of the Brijuni Islands, and a wide range of sports and accommodation options.
reviews: Camp neat and clean. Sanitary facilities are regularly cleaned and toilet paper is never lacking. Staff friendly. The downside is that the WI FI doesn't work, nor does the data network. Not for Telemach users or Telekom users. It's like there's a dead pocket for the signal there. Pizzeria RR is excellent, as is the Mistral bar, where they have top-notch burgers. The downside of these two bars is that payment is only possible with cash and not with a card.
We rented a mobile home and had a wonderful 12 days. We would gladly do it again.

Toilets were super clean! Very friendly welcome. Quite large, but you don't notice that because of the different sections on the grounds. Just outside the campsite is a very long, well-maintained beach with beach bars that seems to go on forever. A bike or moped is recommended, or I think you can also take the bus to explore the surrounding area. The pebble beach in section 'A' does have a lot of large boulders, but just outside the campsite (past the small harbor) is also pleasant. €95 for 2 nights for 2 people with a dog, electricity on the second row with a sea view.

properties.name: Bizantinski kastrum
properties.description: Layered archaeological site on Veliki Brijun with remains of a late antique settlement, Byzantine-period fortifications, and centuries of continuous life within Brijuni National Park.
reviews: This is definitely one of the most interesting attractions on the island. The conservation of some of the stones and structures is very impressive and gives an idea of what life and work was on those times. These are the remains of an ancient settlement from the 4th century, inhabited even during Venetian rule.
An impressive archeological site. Read the info-panel and walk around, try to imagine the life and architecture of this settlement.
Absolutely recommendable for archaeologically interested visitors. There is still so much to see and explore. One of the best archaeological sites on the island.
If you're cycling around the island, would definitely recommend stopping here on the way around - the size and scale of the preserved castle foundations are quite staggering

properties.name: Safari park
properties.description: Family-friendly safari park on Veliki Brijun with exotic and native animals, an ethno park, and a scenic train ride within Brijuni National Park.
reviews: Brijuni Safari Park is one of the most interesting attractions on the island of Veliki (Great) Brijun. Visitors mostly explore the park through a ride on a train and they can see various animals including zebras, ostriches, llamas, and peacocks. There are also sacred Indian cows (zebu), sea turtles and goats. However, the most impressive animal here is the Asian elephant Lanka who arrived on the island in 1972 as a gift from Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi.

In my next life, I want to be born as an animal on Brijuni. The place is so beautiful, with the view on the sea. The grass is green, the animals all seem healthy and happy! Lenka (the elephant) is one of the longest living elephants (she's in her 60s).

I recently had an unforgettable safari experience at the Brijuni Islands in Croatia. The natural beauty of the park is breathtaking, with lush landscapes, diverse wildlife, and stunning views all around. The safari tour was well-organized, and we had the chance to see various animals up close, making it an incredible adventure for nature lovers. One important tip: the ferry ride to the island costs only €10, so make sure you're not overcharged. Be cautious of potential scams, especially from people trying to sell overpriced tickets or unnecessary services outside the official vendors. Other than that, it was a fantastic trip, and I highly recommend it to anyone visiting Croatia!
We really liked our stroll around Brijuni island. The safari park is a nice addition. Definitely check the tickets for the ferry to the island in advance. They sell out fast.

properties.name: Valerijin park
properties.description: Historic triangular park below the Pula Arena, known for its landscaped greenery, Mediterranean plants, and long connection to the city’s urban heritage.
reviews: This is a park located between two major attractions of Pula. There are a playground, some statues, benches to sit down, trees that provide shades, etc.

properties.name: Arena Pula
properties.description: Roman amphitheatre from the 1st century and one of Pula’s most iconic landmarks, known for its monumental architecture, rich history, and major cultural events.
reviews: Incredibly well preserved ruins in a very cool part of town. So much to see around and inside. They also use the middle for various things. They had an ice rink there when I went.

Pula is a small town, so seeing such a grandiose structure from the era of the great Roman Empire here is both unexpected and impressive. There are far fewer visitors than at the Roman Colosseum, so everyone has the unique opportunity to find a secluded spot, stand, and reflect on eternity, on how times, customs, and games have changed over the centuries. Don't miss the small but fascinating museum located in the arena's underground chambers—it's dedicated primarily to olive oil production in Istria.
Gorgeous arena. A must see when in Pula. Beautiful weather helped to get some beautiful shots. More than 2000 years old and one of six biggest amphitheatres still standing.. breathtakingly stunning.

What a site to see! Amazing roman arena built between 27–68 AD. Sixth largest in the world. There is a fee to enter and you are free to walk around once inside. There are QR codes that provide a self guided tour using your phone. Bring ear buds or headphones for the audio. This is one of the best preserved amphitheaters in the world. This is a must see.

properties.name: Fort San Giorgio
properties.description: 19th-century circular hilltop fort in Pula, built as part of the Austro-Hungarian defence system and known for its strong military character.
reviews: A beautiful fort which is worth a 5 star rating! HOWEVER, completely ruined by graffiti and rubbish, see pictures. Government please preserve this amazing object
Nice view, a special scent from the plants when entering, but a little sad that Croatia doesn't take more care of this place that is part of their heritage.

properties.name: Augustov Hram
properties.description: Historic Roman temple on Pula’s Forum, admired for its classical architecture, monumental columns, and lasting connection to the city’s ancient past.
reviews: A remarkably well-preserved gem of Roman architecture, built during the reign of Emperor Augustus, it stands as a graceful reminder of the city’s ancient past. Its elegant columns, finely carved details, and harmonious proportions make it one of the best surviving examples of a Roman temple outside Italy. Inside, the temple houses a small collection of Roman sculptures.

For a couple euro you get to turn back the clock to the time of Christ and envision what the Roman Empire resembled from an architectural perspective and from the statues of that period. Nice little stop in old town Pula.
Loved the temple. Many interesting and beautiful pieces. Good explanation of tenples history. Also, it is a very welcoming stuff that could tell more if you are interested.
The ancient building was bombed during WWII and has been restored with a lot of effort, hard work, and love. 2€ to get in and while it’s small inside it’s a contribution to maintain..

properties.name: Muzej / galerija - Crkva Srca Isusova i Marijina (Sveta Srca)
properties.description: Former church in Pula’s old town, now a museum-gallery space hosting exhibitions, cultural events, and multimedia programmes.
reviews: A little gem hidden in the old town of Pula. Free, quiet, on two levels, eclectic exhibits, special worksheets for children. Loved it.
Free entry, quiet and calm vibes in an old church with multiple levels of art. Would definitely recommend but don't need to spend more than 20-30 minutes here

Free entry. Currently occupied by a selection of highlights from a main art museum in Zagreb, which is undergoing a seismic retrofit. Beautiful space, nicely presented pieces. We were the only people there which is a shame - it deserves more visibility.

properties.name: Park grada Graza
properties.description: Central city park in Pula, created after World War II and known for its shade, archaeological fragments, and relaxed atmosphere near the Forum.
reviews: I love this little hideaway before the forum. Nice place to sit and think about life while under the shade of a small tree
Peaceful park with ancient fragments all over the place among children’s swings makes for an interesting contrast

Fantastic for kids! A couple of advent houses, a small funfair just right for everyone... for both the little ones and the big ones! Enough for everyone! Just right today, not crowded or too many people... The temperature is pleasant for December 2025!

properties.name: Fort Verudella
properties.description: 19th-century coastal fortress on Punta Verudela, now home to Aquarium Pula and known for its military heritage and sea-facing setting.

properties.name: Greenhill
properties.description: Modern one-bedroom apartment in Pula with a terrace, garden, free parking, and airport transfer, located close to the Arena and city centre.
reviews: Very nicely furnished apartment, has everything you need for a good vacation, very friendly hosts, always at hand for any questions.
The apartment itself was equipped with everything I needed for more stay and more. It was located really close to the city which meant getting to it was no issue.
Very nice host. I can only recommend this place. :) Thank you for everything

properties.name: Arheološki muzej Istre
properties.description: Major archaeological museum in Pula focused on Istria’s heritage, with collections and exhibitions across multiple locations while its main building is under renovation.
reviews: Learning something new is amazing. Those tunnels were built long time ago even before my grandpa's time. Just to see that things are not always what seems to be, gives you time to reflect and appreciate what you have now. History is teaching us a good lesson let's don't make the same mistake again. If visit Pula this location gives you a 360 view over the city. The Colosseum is on the East side of the fort.

Pretty great museum if you like history and museums, there's a few rooms on the main level of the citadel. Otherwise, I'd say it's nothing special and the other levels and underground of the citadel are more fascinating.

Hilltop fort in the center of the city. It now houses a museum dedicated, by in large to the occupation of the city, and of Croatia, in second world war. The upper levels provide for excellent view points of the city. It's not expensive to enter and worth a visit during your time in Pula.

properties.name: Amfiteatar
properties.description: Boutique hotel in central Pula, just steps from the Arena, offering modern rooms, a well-known restaurant, and easy access to the old town and waterfront.
reviews: Fantastic hotel, great location, very central. Lovely staff and extremely helpful with suggestions to eat and drink and booking a taxi to the airport. Room 101 was great, really spacious with bathrobes and slippers. Tea and coffee making facilities in the room. Great choice of both hot and cold food at breakfast. Would definitely stay again.
I can’t get over what a great place this is! Location of course, front desk service, comfort, and value. I would highly recommend this facility for any type of visit to Pula.

Great experience staying here! Location is superb and the staff is super friendly. We accidentally broke the tap in the bathroom while staying here, but the hotel staff just exchanged it during the day and it was fixed when we got back in the afternoon. Would definitely recommend staying here!

properties.name: Park Franje Josipa I.
properties.description: Historic waterfront park in Pula, known for its shaded paths, palm trees, family-friendly atmosphere, and relaxed setting near the Riva.
reviews: A beautifully situated park right next to the port of Pula. Thanks to the tall trees, the playground remains shaded even in summer until after midday. A constant sea breeze makes it pleasantly cool despite the high temperatures. Highly recommended. Paid parking is available nearby.
A good place to relax with beautiful views and palm trees all around. There are playgrounds and are close to many tourist attractions in Pula. I recommend it for those with children.
A wonderful park by the harbor in Pula. With plenty of places to relax and a playground for the little ones. Beautifully landscaped and clean.

properties.name: Park Monte Zaro
properties.description: Largest historic park in Pula, set on a hill around the observatory, with fountains, Mediterranean trees, and scenic views over the city and harbour.
reviews: I didn’t expect much, but Montezaro Park turned out to be such a nice surprise. It’s quiet, full and perfect when you need a break from the heat and crowds. I grabbed a coffee and just walked around for a bit…. really relaxing spot with a great local feel.

The roots of Montezaro Park are deeply embedded in Pula’s past. Originally designed in the 19th century, it was a retreat for nobility and a symbol of prestige. Small park that has a fountain, many trails to get around, and plenty of flora areas to enjoy. It's on a small hill so you can get some views of the surrounding area.
A quiet city park on a hill overlooking Pula harbour, a welcome escape far from the madding crowd.

properties.name: Titov park
properties.description: Central waterfront park in Pula, known for its memorial monuments, shaded walking areas, city model, and lively social atmosphere with seasonal events.
reviews: Tito's Park in Pula is a prominent public park that serves as a green oasis in the heart of the city. Named after Josip Broz Tito, the former Yugoslav leader, the park is a popular area for both locals and tourists seeking relaxation and recreation. There's also a miniature model on one side of the park.
Nice park next to the water with many busts of famous partisans, including Tito. The main monument is also dedicated to the partisans. Worth a few pictures.
A piece of more recent Croatian history. Has some statues, a place for kids to play, a few benches and a model of the city.

properties.name: Park kralja Petra Krešimira IV
properties.description: Central city park in Pula with a fountain, Roman mausoleum remains, flower beds, and a peaceful atmosphere close to the waterfront.
reviews: A very nice, well-kept, well-maintained little place in the heart of the city. If you go here, you can sit on one of the small benches and breathe some fresh air while looking at the trees and flowers.

A beautiful little park near Pula's historic center. The fountain in the center, made from a strange rock, is unique and remarkable. Otherwise, this green space is very well-maintained and has a good number of benches for a peaceful rest in the shade.

properties.name: Utvrda Monvidal
properties.description: Historic hilltop fort in Pula with a distinctive horseshoe layout, surrounded by greenery and preserving a raw reminder of the city’s military past.
reviews: Worth checking out but nothing special

The fortress is in very poor condition. It's completely overgrown with trees and grass; you can walk around it in five minutes. There are many interesting stories about it online, but in reality, there's nothing to see. It's a shame such a place is falling into disrepair.
We discovered this fortress by accident during a night walk. Very few people know about it. Despite being neglected, it is a piece of history and worth seeing.

properties.name: Scaletta
properties.description: Quaint hotel in central Pula near the Arena, offering comfortable rooms, breakfast, parking, and easy access to the city’s historic sights.
reviews: Small hotel ,central -5' from the amphitheater ,10' from the historic center of the pleasant town of Pula to walk along the tree-lined Rambla between arches small squares churches from the soaring bell towers to the port.Like the city - bright clean and cozy -so the small hotel in retro style ', slightly French proves safe and fulfilling. It is true that the external stairs lead to the familiar reception and that breakfast is served on the room overlooking the street, -I think a former very chic restaurant whose dishes and crockery now serve an essential breakfast - but the pleasure is given by the central heart just a stone's throw from the Colosseum, magnificent!

We cannot thank the lovely owners enough for all their help with finding a car for us yesterday. The hotel is a real find and value for money and close to the ferry and the city centre. Lovely breakfast also.

properties.name: Park Mladenaca
properties.description: Small central park in Pula near the Forum, known for its landscaped greenery, evening atmosphere, and popularity for wedding photos and sunset walks.
reviews: A great place to watch sunset.
A small park next to the ancient temple.

A small green space near the central Roman Forum and the Temple of Augustus. It's certainly not the best place for jogging or long walks, but it's very well-kept, both for its lawn and trees, and boasts a curious monument located within. It's certainly a great place for a relaxing break in the shade, surrounded by stunning monuments.

properties.name: Hostel Joyce
properties.description: Boutique hostel in the heart of Pula, set beside the Arch of the Sergii and known for its modern rooms, historic character, and walkable location.
reviews: Wonderful hotel with modern rooms, comfortable beds, spectacular views and an unbeatable location. Staff was warm and inviting and had everything prepared for us. Check in and check out was a breeze! Much more affordable than other 4- star hotels in comparable tourist-centric cities (especially during seasonal rush). Will definitely return.
I strongly recommend this hotel: my room was large and clean, Nikola, the boy at the reception desk, was very helpful. Furthermore, guests have a 20 % discount at the adjacent restaurant!

Despite the room being quite small, it was ideal for an overnight stay, was reasonably priced and in a fantastic location. The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful. The room was very clean, quiet and comfortable (the AC was very welcome!). It's in the perfect location to see all the main sights and is also a short walk from restaurants, bars and a bus stop. Thanks for a lovely stay!

I would describe the hotel accommodation as practical, picturesque and modern. Our room was quite small but it was absolutely everything we needed. The bed was very comfortable, the air conditioning was very welcome and the view from the window was of the Triumphal Arch. The hotel is in the centre of Pula, so it is a short walk to the sights, good food and the bus stop to the beach or the station. There is also a great restaurant downstairs for brunch. Thank you very much, the stay was wonderful.

properties.name: Galerija C8
properties.description: Small contemporary gallery in central Pula, run by the Archaeological Museum of Istria, hosting archaeological, artistic, and educational exhibitions.

properties.name: Fort Casoni Vecchi
properties.description: Historic circular hilltop fort in Pula, known for its Austro-Hungarian military heritage, panoramic views, and occasional cultural events.
reviews: The Fort was built in 1852-1854 during the wars; currently stands empty and locked off to visitors. Nothing to see here, overgrown and rather dangerous.

Beautiful structure that unfortunately is abandoned/locked from tourists. The Fort is hidden behind a big building and walls which hides its former beauty. It is in bad shape. Nothing to see here unfortunately.

properties.name: Hotel Pula
properties.description: Large hotel near the beach in Pula, offering comfortable rooms, sports and wellness facilities, and easy access to both the coast and the city’s historic sights.
reviews: It’s hard to choose a hotel based on the reviews, as everyone has an expectation. This hotel is located near the beach, in the middle of a beautiful landscape. Offers parking, wi-fi, gym, swimming pool, breakfast and others. The quaro, though old, was clean and cozy. The service from the staff was excellent, as I had made a reservation that was cancelled and I was surprised at the time, but the boy, very kind, solved the problem. Thank you very much for your attention. The price was a little above the platform, but it was already a little late and we ended up staying there.

This place really needs some love. Sure, lots of options for breakfast, but the visual aspect of spending time here leaves much to be desired. Ancient caprets in the halls tell stories. Not ones I would like to be part of.

It's an old hotel that must have been great in its day. Now it's worn out but makes no pretensions about itself. It has got a nice looking pool but I didn't try it. The rooms are basic and the bathroom is best described as old but functional. Generally the hotel is clean and the staff friendly.

properties.name: Mornarički Park
properties.description: Historic 19th-century park in Pula and the city’s oldest public park, known for its botanical richness, shaded paths, and children’s play areas.
reviews: Very nice park with fountains and children playground. Quiet place near main street, ideal for relax during hiking around Pula.
Great place for stop and chill during walking through Pula.

properties.name: MONTE ZARO
properties.description: Modern hostel in Pula with private bathrooms, breakfast, free parking, and a convenient location close to city attractions.
reviews: We were here in August 2025 And it was perfect, receptionists were always nice and helpfull, the breakfast was delicious, the room was clean, new and quite nice. The location is perfect, we were 10 minutes away from the city center. So basically, i recommend this hostel to anyone, who don’t want to Pay big cash, but do want to spend time in comfort accommodation.
Nice apartment for one night stay. Walking distance from city centre, Arena. Useful kitchen. Good value for money.
Perfect location if you want to explore the city of Pula by foot. The room was clean and the staff was very helpful. Walls are quite thin and you can hear other people in the hall.

properties.name: Villa Carla
properties.description: Spacious villa apartment in Pula near Verudela, offering sea views, a shared pool, parking, and easy access to beaches, restaurants, and city attractions.
reviews: Robert was fantastic. He was extremely welcoming during our stay and helped us with anything we needed. The place was great and easily big enough for the 7 of us. 100% would come back. Thanks Robert

Robert welcomed us on arrival and gave us a tour of the apartment. Such a nice and friendly soul. Very helpful and thoughtful and we made plans to speak to Robert during our stay so he could advise us of places to go and trips to do. Our first evening we spent at a restaurant just a few yards away and his recommendation was spot on. We returned there several times during our stay (Sopranos). Our stay was fantastic - the pool area is relatively small and is sometimes shaded but is a lovely spot. The front of the accommodation has a purpose built bbq area (covered) that we also made good use of a couple of evenings. The local grocery store is a two minute walk away. Go to the butchers section - you’ll be amazed at the quality and price of the fresh meat on offer. A 15 minute walk got us into Pula itself - the nearest beach is 10 minutes away and we were able to have a boat trip a day out in Rovinj and threw some fun on inflatables and quad bikes for good measure. A great stay.

Truly exceptional hosts. So friendly and helpful, we were made to feel like family. The apartment had everything we needed and was a good base location for the things we wanted to explore. Hope to visit again one day!

properties.name: Hostel Art & Joy
properties.description: Friendly hostel in Pula with a garden, terrace, shared kitchen, and convenient access to the Arena and other city attractions.
reviews: We had an amazing stay in this hostel. The staff is very nice and helpful : the receptionist gave us tips on what to see and visit around the city, helped us book bikes. We were also able to change beds to be closer to each other in the room. The bedrooms and common rooms are cleaned daily and the air conditioning was appreciated. The hostel is well located, there are bus stops right next to it, it’s only a 10-minutes walk to the city center. As 2 girls travelling, we felt very confortable and safe staying at this hostel. People we met there were easygoing and we had some really nice times in the kitchen and on the terrace. To sum up : we had a wonderful vacation in Pula :) PS : the only thing that’s missing would be board games accessible to everyone.

We had a nice stay in the private room. Everything is clean, the staff is friendly, pizzeria next door. The cute mosaique and artistic flair got us lost in long conversations about art and the meaning of life ;)

I have stayed in Pula 5 times now. The first time was at an apartment with restricted check in times. 3pm to 5pm only. And a long walk to the centre.I was in a restaurant chatting to someone on the next table.He recommended Art and Joy hostel. I took a walk there the next day. His advice was spot on. I met the manager there and said may I have a look inside, with a possibility of staying there the following year. She agreed. Wow it was great I thought. So I booked for the next Summer. I wasn't disappointed. I have now stayed 4 times each Summer for 4 years.Close to the centre, and the staff are superb. They will help you with any issues you may have.. The hard working cleaner is great also. I recommend it. Choice of rooms to suit your requirements.And good value.. Give it a try for a few nights and then extend it if you want to. Obviously subject to availability.

properties.name: Huguesov park
properties.description: City park in Pula known for its greenery, quiet central setting, and a bust of Nikola Tesla that gives the space memorial significance.
reviews: A lovely little park with a monument to Nikola Tesla.

Great benches and perfect place to come with a book. Best during sunny days as there is a lot of shade and the breeze is nice. Highly recommend for locals or if you are visiting nice break/chill area outside as this area isn’t crowded.

properties.name: Apartmani Pula Arena
properties.description: Modern apartments in central Pula near the Arena, offering equipped kitchens, free WiFi, and easy access to the city’s main sights.
reviews: The apartment is perfectly located (right next to the Arena of Pula) to explore the city. Also it is very close to the ferry terminal which is convenient if you travel onwards with the ship.
The apartment is in an excellent location, in a dilapidated building. The interior furnishings are okay, but there's room for improvement.

Very good location, unfortunately a forest in front obscures the view of the sea. You have to see it from the upper windows. Clean and tidy. The second level of the apartment is like a loft with beds. Quiet and pleasant.

properties.name: SWEET APARTMENT TONY
properties.description: Comfortable apartment in Pula with a terrace, balcony, free private parking, and a fully equipped kitchen, set a short drive from the Arena.
reviews: Family was help-full and kind. Even gave us a homemade cake :) We had all we needs in their apartment. Close to the apartment is shopping mall with all variety of shops. 3 min by car, maybe 10 by foot.

Everything was perfect! The owner was super helpful and friendly! They helped us with bringing the suit see and provided everything we needed. The apartment is super well equipped and kids friendly. Supermarket, city center and beaches are nearby. The best part was AC in this hot summer! Sofa bed could be better but manageable
Very nice apartment with big bathroom, well equipped kitchen, air conditioning in each room, large terrace.

properties.name: Apartments Villa Bubi
properties.description: Comfortable apartments near the beach in Pula, offering terraces, free parking, barbecue facilities, and easy access to the city and Brijuni.
reviews: Great helpful staff, nice and welcoming place. Close to the coast (5-10 minutes on foot), with great beaches. Guarded parking, air conditioning, garden grill. I recommend it to everyone!
If you’re looking for a nice and quiet place to stay, with beautiful garden, this is place for you. Clean, cozy, pet friendly.. great host.. there is a parking lot, bikes to rent..

This apartment is at a beautiful location, near to the beach, with nice and clean, well-equipped rooms (air conditioner, balcony). The host is very friendly and helpful. I would totally recommend to book a room in Villa Bubi! :)

properties.name: Hotel Park Plaza Arena Pula
properties.description: Modern seaside hotel on Punta Verudela in Pula, offering sea views, saltwater pools, wellness facilities, and direct access to the beach in a pine forest setting.
reviews: Lovely hotel, with an excellent buffet breakfast, comfortable rooms, efficient laundry service, and a short walk to the sea, with a beautiful view; we would stay there again

So far, it's been a fantastic holiday. The rooms are quite modern and perfectly adequate for two people. There's a wide selection at breakfast, and the food is very tasty. The staff is always friendly and helpful. We recommend booking bed and breakfast, as there are plenty of restaurants on this small peninsula. What bothers us personally is that no one seems to be monitoring the hotel's sunbathing area. About 50% of the people who lounge there aren't hotel guests, so it's impossible to find a spot by lunchtime. You're basically forced to reserve sunbeds early in the morning.
What a beautiful location! The beds were very comfortable. The breakfast was amazing!

properties.name: Arena Stoja
properties.description: Seaside campsite on the Stoja peninsula in Pula, offering sea-view pitches, a peaceful natural setting, and direct access to the rocky coast.
reviews: Beautiful campsite on the Adriatic coast. We watched the dolphins in the evenings, which was very impressive. There's a very good restaurant on site, and everything is very clean. The staff at reception are very friendly, and the sanitary facilities are constantly cleaned. The bus to Pula stops right in front of the campsite, and you can also easily walk to the Colosseum in 35 minutes. We really enjoyed our stay here and would love to come back. The only minor drawback is that, unlike most campsites, there are no dog waste bags available anywhere on the grounds. A dog costs €7 per day.

During our Istria tour in April 2026, we stayed here for two nights in our campervan. Five stars are well-deserved. Everything was clean and very nice. We were parked in the first row right by the sea and enjoyed the sunset. It was very quiet at night, and the staff were very friendly. We would gladly come back.
Beautiful campsite by the sea. Clean and excellent sanitary facilities. Friendly welcome. Many facilities. Good price.

properties.name: Park žrtava fašizma
properties.description: Neighbourhood park in Šijana, Pula, with a children’s playground, benches, and a pleasant green space for everyday relaxation.

properties.name: Fort Turtian
properties.description: Historic polygonal fortress near Pula, built as part of the city’s defensive ring and still standing prominently above the surrounding landscape.
reviews: A beautiful  Austro-Hungarian fortress with a beautiful sunset over Pula.

We were there at the end of September. The only visitors to the fort were a few goats. The gate was closed to everyone else. We walked all the way around, and there's definitely no access. There's a sign at the entrance stating that the army abandoned the fort in 1992, and after that, it gradually fell into disrepair due to vandalism. Now, some local people have come together to clear out the fort, and perhaps there will be an opportunity to visit it on special days. There were no official opening hours listed. So, based on current information, it's not worth the trip.

properties.name: Park pod Arenom
properties.description: Historic landscaped park beneath the Pula Arena, known for its flower beds, elegant Italian-era design, and one of the city’s most photogenic settings.

properties.name: Slavoluk Sergijevaca
properties.description: Historic Roman arch in central Pula, known as the Arch of the Sergii or Golden Gate, famous for its ornate reliefs and long-standing civic significance.
reviews: The Golden Gate in Pula is a beautifully preserved historic landmark. Its Roman architecture and detailed stonework make it a must-see in the city center. A great spot for a quick walk, photos, and soaking in the ancient atmosphere.

A Roman triumphal arch, beautifully intact through the ages. Its intricate carvings reflect the artistry of the Augustan era, while its enduring form stands as a testament to the power and legacy of ancient Rome.
Beautiful Roman arch in the heart of Pula. The Arch of the Sergii is a stunning reminder of the city's ancient past. Great spot for photos and easy to find while walking through the old town.

Another beautiful piece of history which must be seen to be appreciated when you’re in Pula. Seems to be the main entrance essentially to the late ish night shops and restaurants/bars etc. Did have a nice jazz band situated here one night too!

properties.name: Dvojna vrata
properties.description: Ancient Roman city gate in Pula with two arched passages, notable stone decoration, and direct links to some of the city’s key archaeological sites.
reviews: Nice looking arch together with the walls in the heart of Pula. Reminds me like I'm in Italy. However, it would just need to be cleaned from the black patina that has accumulated on it over time so it can look even nicer.
Indeed, the two gates look identical. The fact that they are about a thousand years old is fascinating.
A pair of twelfe entrances in ancient roman and mediaval Pula...but if you want to know more - hire a local licenced tour guide...

properties.name: Grand Hotel Brioni Pula
